mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with the interaction term coefficient insignificant at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values indicate expanding manufacturing capacity utilization that directly supports earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during mid-cycle expansion yield negative 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor market expansion that supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day SPX direction, with Rank IC < 0.01 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPI spikes above 0.4% are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales validates synchronized economic expansion across production and consumption sectors, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between GDPC1 and RSAFS growth rates fails to improve out-of-sample return prediction accuracy by > 2% over baseline models.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a recessionary phase signals collapsing manufacturing output that directly reduces corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during recessions are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in > 50% of observations.
